name: Hatchet (Durable Task Queue / Workflow Engine)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Hatchet Cloud is commercial (managed server)

auth.notes: API key for Hatchet Cloud or self-hosted server

performance.notes: Task queue overhead; actual task execution time varies

agent_readiness.notes: Modern durable task queue with workflow DAGs. Python and TypeScript SDKs. @hatchet.workflow() decorator for defining workflows as DAGs. Durable execution (automatic retries, failure recovery). Rate limiting, concurrency control, scheduling. Web UI for monitoring runs. Cron schedules. Child workflow spawning. Event-driven triggers. Alternative to Temporal (Temporal is more mature), Inngest (serverless-first), Prefect (data-pipeline-first). Hatchet targets general-purpose backend jobs. Self-host via Docker or use Hatchet Cloud.

gotchas: Less mature ecosystem than Temporal or Celery
Requires running Hatchet server (self-host or cloud)
Smaller community and fewer integrations than alternatives
best_when: Backend jobs needing durable execution with workflow DAGs
Python or TypeScript services with complex multi-step workflows
Teams wanting simpler alternative to Temporal
Applications needing rate limiting and concurrency control for tasks
avoid_when: Data pipeline use cases (Prefect or Dagster better)
Simple queue needs (Celery or BullMQ simpler)
summary: Hatchet is a modern open-source durable task queue with workflow DAG support. @hatchet.workflow() decorator, automatic retries, concurrency control, cron scheduling, and child workflows. Python and TypeScript SDKs. Web UI for monitoring. Self-host via Docker or use Hatchet Cloud. Simpler API than Temporal but less mature. Best for backend jobs needing reliable durable execution without Temporal's operational complexity.
